headline: OpenAI partners with the Pentagon for military use of AI technologies.

items.title: OpenAI's 'compromise' with the Pentagon is what Anthropic feared
items.summary: OpenAI has struck a deal with the Pentagon to allow the use of its AI technologies in classified settings. This comes after a public reprimand of Anthropic by the Pentagon. The negotiations were reportedly rushed, and OpenAI emphasized the importance of maintaining ethical standards while engaging in this partnership. The deal highlights the growing intersection of AI capabilities with military applications, raising ethical and privacy concerns.
items.why_it_matters: Tech leaders should monitor this development as it underscores the increasing role of AI in national security and the potential ethical implications.

items.title: Launch HN: Cekura (YC F24) – Testing and monitoring for voice and chat AI agents
items.summary: Cekura introduces a platform for simulating and monitoring voice and chat AI agent interactions. The service has been operational for 1.5 years, expanding from voice to chat simulations. It aims to provide realistic user interaction scenarios to enhance the development and testing of AI agents.

items.title: alibaba/OpenSandbox
items.summary: OpenSandbox is a versatile platform providing a sandbox environment for AI applications. It supports multi-language SDKs, unified APIs, and Docker/Kubernetes runtimes, making it suitable for coding agents, GUI agents, and AI code execution.

items.title: AI-generated art can't be copyrighted (Supreme Court declines review)
items.summary: The Supreme Court has declined to review a case involving the copyrightability of AI-generated art, effectively ruling that such works cannot be copyrighted. This decision clarifies the legal standing of AI-generated content in terms of intellectual property rights.

items.title: Physicists developing a quantum computer that’s entirely open source
items.summary: A team of physicists is working on a quantum computer that is completely open source. This initiative aims to democratize access to quantum computing technologies by allowing researchers and developers to explore and contribute to its development.

items.title: Meta’s AI smart glasses and data privacy concerns
items.summary: Meta's new AI smart glasses have sparked discussions over data privacy, as workers express concerns over the extent of data that can be collected and monitored through these devices.

all_items.title: Building specialized AI without sacrificing intelligence: Nova Forge data mixing in action
all_items.summary: In this post, we share results from the AWS China Applied Science team's comprehensive evaluation of Nova Forge using a challenging Voice of Customer (VOC) classification task, benchmarked against open-source models.

all_items.summary: This post explores how to build an intelligent conversational agent using Amazon Bedrock, LangGraph, and managed MLflow on Amazon SageMaker AI.

all_items.summary: On June 1, 2023, as a sweltering heat wave baked Quebec, thousands of lightning strikes flashed across the province, setting off more than 120 wildfires. all_items.title: Show HN: I built a sub-500ms latency voice agent from scratch
all_items.summary:
I built a voice agent from scratch that averages ~400ms end-to-end latency (phone stop → first syllable). That’s with full STT → LLM → TTS in the loop, clean barge-ins, and no precomputed responses.

What moved the needle:
